content.full_text:
May 16, 1990

VIA FAX AND FIRST CLASS MAIL

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Attn: Deanne Stone

Re: Opinion Request on Behalf of W. Brad Parton, Mayor, Redondo Beach, California

Dear Ms. Stone:

In response to our telephone conversation of May 16, 1990, this letter will formally advise you that the request for opinion dated May 8, 1990, by my office was made at the request of W. Brad Parton, Mayor of the City of Redondo Beach.

The name of the partnership which originally owned the building was Phelan Associates, a limited partnership. Mayor Parton and Robert Scribner were the general partners, holding 50% interests. Mayor Parton and Mr. Scribner were individual co-trustors on the deed of trust in the amount of $532,000.00. When the property was sold, Mayor Parton and Mr. Scribner took back a purchase money deed of trust in the amount of $85,000.00. They are each equal individual beneficiaries of said deed of trust.

The Rental Rehabilitation Loan Program is funded separately from the Section 8 program. However, an applicant who is approved for the Rental Rehabilitation Loan Program is required, but not obligated, to accept qualifying Section 8 applicants. The present owner has 3 Section 8 tenants out of a total of 10 units. The vacancy rate in the area is approximately 3%-4%. The applicant is seeking a loan of $28,000. The total amount to be spent rehabilitating the property will be $84,000. The remaining $56,000 will come from sources other than the City.

The present Rental Rehabilitation Loan Program contract expires July 31. The applicant will be funded from the existing program.

Fair Political Practices Commission  
May 16, 1990  
Page 2

The Section 8 program is to be considered for renewal on or about June 1992.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]
Gordon C. Phillips  
City Attorney

GCP:dp  
cc: Mayor Brad Parton
